children.text: the term “at-risk” has the meaning given that term in ;section 6472 of title 20

children.label: A
children.text: a unit of local government that is in compliance with the requirements of part B of subchapter II; or

children.label: B
children.text: a nonprofit organization in partnership with a unit of local government described in subparagraph (A);

children.label: A
children.text: alcohol and substance abuse prevention or treatment services;

children.label: B
children.text: tutoring and remedial education, especially in reading and mathematics;

children.label: C
children.text: child and adolescent health and mental health services;

children.label: D
children.text: recreation services;

children.label: E
children.text: leadership and youth development activities;

children.label: F
children.text: the teaching that individuals are and should be held accountable for their actions;

children.label: G
children.text: assistance in the development of job training skills;

children.label: H
children.text: youth mentoring programs;

children.label: I
children.text: after-school programs;

children.label: i
children.text: early childhood development services;

children.label: ii
children.text: voluntary home visiting programs;

children.label: iii
children.text: nurse-family partnership programs;

children.label: iv
children.text: parenting skills training;

children.label: v
children.text: child abuse prevention programs;

children.label: vi
children.text: family stabilization programs;

children.label: vii
children.text: child welfare services;

children.label: viii
children.text: family violence intervention programs;

children.label: ix
children.text: adoption assistance programs;

children.label: x
children.text: emergency, transitional and permanent housing assistance;

children.label: xi
children.text: job placement and retention training;

children.label: xii
children.text: summer jobs programs;

children.label: xiii
children.text: alternative school resources for youth who have dropped out of school or demonstrate chronic truancy;

children.label: xiv
children.text: conflict resolution skill training;

children.label: xv
children.text: restorative justice programs;

children.label: xvi
children.text: mentoring programs;

children.label: xvii
children.text: targeted gang prevention, intervention and exit services;

children.label: xviii
children.text: training and education programs for pregnant teens and teen parents; and

children.label: xix
children.text: pre-release, post-release, and reentry services to assist detained and incarcerated youth with transitioning back into and reentering the community; and

children.label: K
children.text: other data-driven evidence-based or promising prevention programs;

children.label: A
children.text: not fewer than 15 and not more than 21 members; and1So in original. The word “and” probably should not appear.1

children.label: i
children.text: public agencies and private nonprofit organizations serving juveniles and their families; and

children.label: ii
children.text: business and industry;

children.label: C
children.text: at least one representative of the faith community, one adjudicated youth, and one parent of an adjudicated youth; and

children.label: D
children.text: in the case of an eligible entity described in paragraph (1)(B), a representative of the nonprofit organization of the eligible entity;
the term “mentoring” means matching 1 adult with 1 or more youths for the purpose of providing guidance, support, and encouragement through regularly scheduled meetings for not less than 9 months;
the term “State advisory group” means the advisory group appointed by the chief executive officer of a State under a plan described in ; andsection 11133(a) of this title
the term “State entity” means the State agency designated under or the entity receiving funds under .section 11133(a)(1) of this titlesection 11133(d) of this title
